patch.value:
## Agent need
The philosophy requires that an agent rebuild full working context from the pm CLI alone, which is only possible if every item's metadata is complete, honest, and machine-checkable.

## Delivered surface (pm-33cw)
core/governance (content-fields, issue-codes, metadata-coverage); core/validate checks + fix-planning; the remediation registry surfaced via pm health --json / pm validate --fix-hints.

## Verified by
metadata-coverage, issue-codes, and content-fields tests. Distinct from pm-sz6w (the narrow remediation-command story); this is the capability-level statement for pm-33cw.

As an agent gating on tracker quality, I want pm validate and pm health to surface every data-quality gap with a machine-executable remediation, so that I can keep context trustworthy and rebuild it from pm alone

Each validate/health warning maps to a remediation hint and an open item; metadata-coverage/issue-code/content-field predicates are accurate (no false positives, no missed real gaps); no placeholder resolution or close_reason evidence is ever written.
Delivered and maintained by pm-33cw: validate/health checks, metadata-coverage primitives, and a machine-executable remediation registry; hardening tracked by open children.
An agent keeps the tracker trustworthy and rebuilds full context from pm alone.
validate/health + coverage predicates + remediation registry shipped; every warning maps to an open remediation item.
Delivered and maintained by pm-33cw: validate/health checks, metadata-coverage primitives, and a machine-executable remediation registry; hardening tracked by open children.
